Bruce Feder, Esq., for Hiroshi Onodera, Beneficiary.

At the outset, the court admonishes Mr. Cooper for not clearing the issues that have been pending for several months. At the court's query, Mr. Cooper reports of having an accounting ready and requests sixty (60) days to prepare, finalize, and formally file the accounting. No objection to Mr. Cooper's request is presented. The court advises that it is inclined to take the instant petition off calendar and set a status on close of estate. Off Calendar Jul-09-2024 Continued To Aug-27-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Armon Cooper appeared by zoom for and with Elizabeth Onodera (administrator) by zoom.
Zachary McCoy appeared by zoom for Leo Bautista (successor trustee).
Bruce Feder (guardian ad litem) appeared by zoom for Horoshi Ondera (beneficiary).

The court stated that the request for extraordinary fees is premature, and that Mr. Cooper has not substantiated his extraordinary fee request sufficiently, but it is willing to advance his request as partial statutory fees. Mr. Cooper stated that he would be grateful for the advance, that all that is left to do is to file the tax returns and to complete the accounting and requested a two-month continuance. Mrs. Onodera stated that the probate accountant has been dealing with some serious medical issues, that she may need to get another probate accountant and that she has all of the necessary documents. The First and Final Report of Administrator on Waiver of Account, etc. is continued to August 27, 2024, at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The court will revise the proposed order.

Off Calendar Aug-09-2022 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Armon Cooper, for and with Elizabeth Onodera, via Zoom, and Hiroki Onodera, Suspended Co-Administrators; Bruce Feder, via Zoom, Guardian ad Litem; Janet Boessenecker, via Zoom, for the San Francisco Public Administrator (SFPA), Interested Party.

Mr. Cooper advises that a Petition to Appoint a Successor Trustee has been filed in the related Trust matter with a hearing date in December. Mr. Cooper offers advocacy on behalf of his client for her appointment as Administrator as appointing the SFPA would add superfluous expense and time. Mr. Cooper advises that the parties have made attempts to agree to appoint third party fiduciary Andrea Leung. The court reminds Mr. Cooper that this matter has been delayed for several years now. Mr. Feder confirms discussions regarding the appointment of Andrea Leung and believes that she might be interested in serving as Administrator. The court reiterates its position regarding the several delays in this matter and that the will states that a third party is to serve as fiduciary and does not name either of the decedent's children to serve in that capacity and that it needs to appoint an Administrator to close this matter. Ms. Onodera offers testimony regarding her efforts with this case and of the issues that caused the delays. Ms. Boessenecker offers testimony regarding the SFPA's position regarding its possible appointment as Administrator and advises that the SFPA would defer the appointment if someone else consents to serving. After additional discussion and consideration, Mr. Feder advises that he intends to monitor this matter closely if the court is inclined to appoint Ms. Onodera as Administrator. The court advises that it is intends to admit the will and appoint Ms. Onodera as Administrator with Will Annexed. As a technical matter, the court is removing both suspended Co-Administrators Elizabeth Onodera and Hiroki Onodera. There being no objections, the Court granted the Petition for Letters of Administration With Will Annexed. The will dated Apr-11-1994 is admitted to probate. Elizabeth Onodera is appointed Administrator with limited authority. Bond is not required. Karen Ann Simons is appointed probate referee. Order for the same signed in open Court. Status on close of estate is set for Aug-15-2023 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

The Status for Referral to the SFPA is taken off calendar. The Status of Appointment of a Temporary Successor Trustee is continued to Dec-14-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Bastain. Not Reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Inventory And Appraisal
Off Calendar Jun-07-2022 Continued To Aug-09-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Armon Cooper, via Zoom, for and with Elizabeth Onodera, and Hiroki Onodera, Personal Representatives; Bruce Feder, via Zoom, Guardian ad Litem; Janet Boessenecker, via Zoom, for the San Francisco Public Administrator, Interested Party.

The court advises Mr. Cooper that he needs to file form DE-147S for Hiroki Onodera. The court queries Mr. Cooper as to the status of a final Inventory and Appraisal ("I&A"). Mr. Cooper offers testimony as to the reason why he has yet to file the I&A. The court, Mr. Cooper, and Ms. Onodera discuss the status of the property located in Kern County. The court points to the uncertainty of whether the decedent owned 100% of the Kern County property at the time of his death. The court and Ms. Onodera discuss her whereabouts at the time of her mother's death and points to the declaration regarding the same not including a statement from Hiroki. Mr. Cooper advises that he intends to file a declaration regarding the same from Hiroki, for the record. The court points to the fact that if the court were to admit the will, it calls for the appointment of a corporate fiduciary who has refused to serve as trustee and there is no mention of a successor trustee. Mr. Cooper believes that the children should have priority. The court advises that it does not intend to appoint a representative until a trustee, preferably a professional fiduciary, is in place. Ms. Onodera reports of contacting several banks regarding the preceding and that she has been advised that banks will not serve as trustee without a establishing an account of at least $3 million dollars. The court advises that the parties need to find a professional fiduciary if a corporate one cannot be secured and reminds Mr. Cooper that a petition for appointment of either a professional or corporate fiduciary is required and that he should meet and confer regarding this issue. The court advises Mr. Cooper and his clients to make this matter a priority and that if a Petition for Appointment of a Temporary Successor Trustee is not filed by the next calling of this matter, the court will strongly consider appointing the San Francisco Public Administrator.

After additional discussion, all matters are continued to Aug-09-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. A Status on Appointment of a Temporary Successor Trustee is set for the same date.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Bastain. Not Reported.

The court begins by confirming with Mr. Cooper that Administrator Elizabeth Onodera continues to distribute assets from the estate. The court then advises of its concerns regarding the spending of estate assets. The court then mentions the petition for order confirming successor trustee in the related trust matter which was denied by the court. The court then reminds Mr. Cooper regarding the initial petition for letters of administration in this matter showing that estate assets totaled more than ten million dollars and that to date, an inventory and appraisal has yet to be filed. Mr. Cooper reports that an inventory and appraisal was never filed because there are no assets to report. The court advises Mr. Cooper that an inventory and appraisal should be filed, regardless. After additional discussion, the court advises Mr. Cooper that it is suspending the Personal Representatives. A hearing for an Order to Show Cause as to why the Personal Representatives should not be removed will be set. The court then confirms with Mr. Cooper that his clients no longer have authorization to use estate assets. The court is also ordering an accounting to cover the dates from Nov-21-2012 to Nov-20-2017 and for the filing of an inventory and appraisal. Mr. Feder and the court then discuss the need to appoint a Special Administrator considering the suspension of the Personal Representatives. Mr. Feder advises that he intends to discuss the preceding with Mr. Armon and act accordingly.

The Petition for Termination, etc. is continued to Nov-16-2021 at 9:00 am in Department 204. An Order to Show Cause as to why the Personal Representatives should not be removed is set for the same date.
